cover.title: Taxation (Annual Rates for 2025–26, Compliance Simplification, and Remedial Measures) Act 2026

front.enactment: The Parliament of New Zealand enacts as follows:

prov.heading: Title

para.text: This Act is the Taxation (Annual Rates for 2025–26, Compliance Simplification, and Remedial Measures) Act 2026.

prov.heading: Commencement

para.text: This Act comes into force on 31 March 2026, except as provided in this section.
Section 170 comes into force on 10 October 2000.
Section 86 comes into force on 1 January 2009.
Sections 87 and 131(20)﻿(a), (35)﻿(a), (43)﻿(a), and (45) come into force on 1 April 2010.
Section 144(4) comes into force on 21 June 2010.
Section 192(2) and (3) comes into force on 1 August 2010.
Sections 148(5) and (11) and 152(1) and (4) come into force on 1 April 2011.
Section 144(5) comes into force on 13 August 2013.
Sections 33 and 39(2) and (4) come into force on 1 April 2017.
Section 144(6) comes into force on 18 April 2017.
Sections 63(1)﻿(a) and 66 come into force on 1 July 2018.
Section 213(3)﻿(d) and (e) comes into force on 18 March 2019.
Section 252(2) comes into force on 1 April 2019.
Section 88 comes into force on 1 April 2020.
Section 214(2) and (3) comes into force on 1 April 2021.
Section 245 comes into force on 30 March 2022.
Section 118(3), (4), (5), (6), (7), and (8) comes into force on 1 April 2022.
Section 144(7) and (8) comes into force on 7 December 2022.
Sections 151(1), 167, and 175(1) come into force on 1 April 2023.
Section 116 comes into force on 1 April 2024.
Sections 80 and 127 come into force on 1 July 2024.
Section 214(1) comes into force on 31 July 2024.
Sections 131(7), (20)﻿(b), and (43)﻿(b) and (c), 134, and 135 come into force on 26 August 2024.
Section 145 comes into force on 21 February 2025.
Section 144(9) comes into force on 5 March 2025.
Sections 8(1) and (3), 24, 25, 29, 117, 118(1) and (2), 119, 120(1), and 131(4) and (16) come into force on 16 April 2025.
Sections 5, 40, 41, 42, 43, 44, 45, 46, 53, 54, and 131(41) come into force on 22 May 2025.
Section 99 comes into force on 1 July 2025.
Section 144(11) comes into force on 16 July 2025.
Section 144(12) comes into force on 30 July 2025.
Section 193(3) comes into force on 26 August 2025.
Section 213(3)﻿(c) comes into force on 1 October 2025.
Sections 91, 100, and 131(2) and (37) come into force on 26 December 2025.
Sections 19(2) and 131(38) come into force on 12 January 2026.
Sections 113, 114, 152(3), and 218(2) come into force on 21 February 2026.
Sections 244 and 246(2) come into force on 31 March 2026.
Section 213(2) and (4) comes into force on 1 July 2026.
Section 143(2) comes into force on 1 April 2028.
Section 105(2) comes into force on a single date set by Order in Council.
If section 105(2) has not come into force by 1 April 2027, it comes into force then.
An Order in Council made under subsection (43) is secondary legislation (see Part 3 of the Legislation Act 2019 for publication requirements).

legtable.summary: The following table is small in size and has 3 columns that are grouped under the heading Legislation Act 2019 requirements for secondary legislation made under this section.

entry.emphasis: Legislation Act 2019 requirements for secondary legislation made under this section

row.entry: PCO must publish it on the legislation website and notify it in the Gazette
The Minister must present it to the House of Representatives
It may be disallowed by the House of Representatives

entry.emphasis: This note is not part of the Act.

history.history-note: Section 2(1): editorial change made by the PCO, on 22 April 2026, under sections 86(1) and 87(m) of the Legislation Act 2019 (2019 No 58).

prov.heading: Annual rates of income tax for 2025–26 tax year

para.text: Income tax imposed by section BB 1 (Imposition of income tax) of the Income Tax Act 2007 must, for the 2025–26 tax year, be paid at the basic rates specified in schedule 1 of that Act.

prov.heading: Amendments to Income Tax Act 2007

para.text: This Part amends the Income Tax Act 2007.

amends-note.amends-text: See section 2(43) and (44), if section 105(2) has not come into force earlier by OIC it comes into force on this date

prov.heading: Section CC 15 replaced (New investment assets: change of use)

para.text: Replace section CC 15 with:

prov.label: CC 15
prov.heading: New investment assets: change of use

para.text: This section applies, for a person and a new investment asset, when—
the person has deducted an amount (the deducted DI 5 amount) for expenditure incurred in acquiring the asset under section DI 5 (New investment asset deduction) for an income year; and
the person changes their use of the asset in a subsequent income year; and

the change of use means the amount of the deduction the person would have been allowed under section DI 5 if the section were applied as if the income year were the income year in which the change of use occurs is less than the deducted DI 5 amount.

prov.body.subprov.crosshead: When this section does not apply: less than 25% change in use

para.text: This section does not apply when the amount calculated using the following formula, expressed as a percentage, is less than 25%:
The person has an amount of income equal to the amount calculated using the following formula:

eqn.eqn-line: existing DI 5 deduction − new DI 5 deduction.

para.text: In the formulas in subsections (2) and (3),—

new DI 5 deduction is the amount the person would have been allowed as a deduction under section DI 5 if the section were applied as if the income year referred to in the section were the income year in which the change of use occurs:
existing DI 5 deduction is,—
if subparagraph (ii) does not apply, the deducted DI 5 amount:
if this section has previously been applied to the asset, the amount that was the new DI 5 deduction amount in the formula on the preceding application of this section.
For an asset that is depreciable property, the amount of income under subsection (3) increases the asset’s adjusted tax value for the purposes of section EE 48(1)﻿(a) (Effect of disposal or event).

Thomas buys a new yacht for $100,000 that he begins using in the 2025–26 income year. He determines that the yacht will be used 90% of the time for business use and chooses to claim a new investment asset deduction on this basis. He therefore claims $18,000 as a new investment asset deduction, which is 90% of the full $20,000 deduction that was available to him under section DI 5. However, he reduces the cost or adjusted tax value used for determining depreciation of the yacht by $20,000 in accordance with section DI 6. In the 2026–27 income year, he only uses the yacht 40% of the time for business. This is a change of use of 25% or more. Had Thomas taken the new investment asset deduction in the 2026–27 income year, he would only have been entitled to claim $8,000, being 40% of the full deduction available. Thomas must return income of $10,000 ($18,000 − $8,000) in the 2026–27 income year. The asset’s adjusted tax value is increased by $10,000 accordingly.

Adam buys a commercial property, comprising land and a commercial building, for $15 million in the 2025–26 income year. The cost of the building is $10 million. He claims $2 million as a new investment asset deduction in that income year, and the cost or adjusted tax value of the building is reduced to $8 million. In the 2027–28 income year, Adam decides to rent out the top floor of the building as residential accommodation. This constitutes 25% of the floor space of the building. This is a change of use of 25% or more. Had Adam taken the new investment asset deduction in the 2027–28 income year, the building would only have been a new investment asset to the extent of the 75% of the building that was not a dwelling. Therefore, Adam would only have been able to claim a new investment asset deduction of $1.5 million, being 75% of the full deduction available. Adam must return income of $500,000 ($2 million − $1.5 million) in the 2027–28 income year. The adjusted tax value of the building is increased by $500,000 accordingly.

ird.aids.term.list: adjusted tax value, amount, commercial building, deduction, depreciable property, dwelling, income, income year, land, new investment asset

prov.heading: Section CD 36 amended (Foreign investment fund income)

para.text: After section CD 36(1)﻿(b)﻿(iv), insert:
the revenue account method; and
In section CD 36, list of defined terms, insert revenue account method.

prov.heading: Section CD 44 amended (Available capital distribution amount)

para.text: In section CD 44(1), in the formula, replace capital gains with net capital amount and delete − capital losses.
Replace section CD 44(2)﻿(c) with:

net capital amount is the total of the capital gain amounts less the capital loss amounts arising in the 1992–93 tax year or a later tax year that is available for distribution to shareholders in the company on liquidation but excluding any gain or loss occurring when the company distributes property to shareholders on the liquidation:
Repeal section CD 44(2)﻿(f).
Replace section CD 44(8) with:

amend.subprov.crosshead: Net capital gain: amalgamated company inheriting net capital gain

para.text: An amalgamated company is treated as deriving, at the time of the amalgamation, an amount (the net amount) equal to the total of the capital gain amounts, less capital loss amounts arising in the 1992–93 tax year or a later tax year, of an amalgamating company to the extent to which—
the amalgamating company’s net amount was available for distribution at the time of the amalgamation; and
was not distributed to any person other than the amalgamated company.

amend.subprov.crosshead: Exception when amalgamated and amalgamating company the same

para.text: Subsection (8) does not apply to an amalgamated company if the amalgamated company is the same company as the amalgamating company.
Replace section CD 44(10)﻿(a) with:

the total of the capital gain amounts arising after 31 March 1988 that are available for distribution to shareholders in the company on the liquidation, excluding any gain occurring when the company distributes property to a shareholder on the liquidation; and
Subsections (1) to (5) apply for the 2008–09 and later income years.

prov.heading: Section CE 1 amended (Amounts derived in connection with employment)

para.text: After section CE 1(2), insert:

amend.subprov.crosshead: Meaning of benefit in money

para.text: In this section, benefit in money does not include a gift card unless—
the employer chooses to treat the provision of a gift card to an employee as an amount derived in connection with their employment; or
the provision of the card has a purpose or effect of defeating the application of the Child Support Act 1991.
After section CE 1(3B), insert:

amend.subprov.crosshead: Reimbursement of employee expenditure for benefit

para.text: For the treatment of a reimbursement payment made to an employee for incurring expenditure for a benefit, see section CE 1BA.
In section CE 1, list of defined terms, insert benefit in money, employment, and gift card.

prov.heading: New section CE 1BA inserted (Reimbursement of employee expenditure for benefit)

para.text: After section CE 1, insert:

prov.label: CE 1BA
prov.heading: Reimbursement of employee expenditure for benefit

para.text: This section applies when an employer pays an amount to reimburse an employee for expenditure the employee has incurred for a benefit that, if the employer provided it directly to the employee, would constitute an unclassified benefit under section CX 37 (Meaning of unclassified benefit).
The amount paid by the employer is, at the employer’s election, either—
employment income of the employee under section CE 1; or
an unclassified benefit under section CX 37.

If the employer chooses to treat the amount paid as employment income of the employee, as described in subsection (2)﻿(a), section CW 17BA (Reimbursement of expenditure paid as employment income) applies to the payment.

prov.body.subprov.crosshead: Exclusion: payments for certain work-related meals

para.text: Subsection (2) does not apply when an employer pays an amount to reimburse an employee for expenditure to which section CW 17CB (Payments for certain work-related meals) applies and the time limit in subsection (5) of that section has elapsed.

ird.aids.term.list: amount, employee, employer, employment income, pay, unclassified benefit

prov.heading: Section CE 7B amended (Meaning of share scheme taxing date)

para.text: After section CE 7B(2), insert:

amend.subprov.crosshead: Meaning for employee deferred shares

para.text: Despite subsections (1) and (2), if the shares are employee deferred shares under section EA 4B (Deferred tax for unlisted employee share schemes), the share scheme taxing date is the liquidity event date.
In section CE 7B, list of defined terms, insert employee deferred shares, liquidity event date, and share scheme taxing date.
Subsections (1) and (2) apply to shares issued or transferred by a company under an employee share scheme on or after 1 April 2026.

prov.heading: Section CF 1 amended (Benefits, pensions, compensation, and government grants)

para.text: In section CF 1(2), in the definition of pension, paragraph (a), replace in return for services that the person with or a trustee of their estate if that person and replace provided to with provided services to.
In section CF 1(2), in the definition of pension, replace paragraph (b) with:
does not include a payment made to—
the person because of, and within 1 year after, the death of that parent, child, spouse, civil union partner or de facto partner, former spouse, civil union partner or de facto partner, or dependant:
a trustee of the person’s estate because of, and within 1 year after, the person’s death.

prov.heading: New section CH 10C inserted (Interest apportionment: Non-qualifying infrastructure debt)

para.text: After section CH 10B, insert:

prov.label: CH 10C
prov.heading: Interest apportionment: non-qualifying infrastructure debt

para.text: An amount derived by a person under section FE 7C(4) (Exemption for eligible infrastructure) is income of the person.

ird.aids.term.list: amount, income

para.text: Subsection (1) applies for the 2026–27 and later income years.

prov.heading: Section CQ 5 amended (When FIF income arises)

para.text: After section CQ 5(1)﻿(c)﻿(xiv), insert:
the exemption for share users in returning share transfers in section EX 43B (Exemption for share users in returning share transfers):
In section CQ 5(1)﻿(g), replace EX 56 with EX 56B.
In section CQ 5, list of defined terms, insert returning share transfer and share user.

prov.heading: Section CV 19 amended (Additional income for certain imputation credits)

para.text: After section CV 19(2)﻿(b), insert:
the revenue account method.
In section CV 19, list of defined terms, insert revenue account method.

prov.heading: Section CW 17 amended (Expenditure on account, and reimbursement of, employees)

para.text: In section CW 17(5), after CW 16F,, insert CW 17BA,.

prov.heading: New section CW 17BA inserted (Reimbursement of expenditure paid as employment income)

para.text: After section CW 17, insert:

prov.label: CW 17BA
prov.heading: Reimbursement of expenditure paid as employment income

para.text: This section applies when an employer chooses to treat an amount paid to an employee under section CE 1BA (Reimbursement of employee expenditure for benefit) as employment income.

An amount paid as employment income under section CE 1BA(2)﻿(a) is exempt income of the employee to the extent to which the payment would not have exceeded the threshold limits in section RD 45 (Unclassified benefits and gift cards) had the payment been treated as an unclassified benefit under section CX 37 (Meaning of unclassified benefit).

ird.aids.term.list: amount, employee, employer, employment income, exempt income, unclassified benefit

prov.heading: Section CW 19 amended (Amounts derived during short-term visits)

para.text: In section CW 19(3), after section, insert and sections CW 22B and CW 22C.

prov.heading: New sections CW 22B to CW 22D inserted

para.text: After section CW 22, insert:

prov.label: CW 22B
prov.heading: Amounts derived by non-resident visitors during visits

para.text: Income a non-resident visitor derives from performing personal or professional services in New Zealand during a visit is exempt income if—
the services are performed for or on behalf of a person who is not resident in New Zealand; and

the amount derived from the personal or professional services is liable to tax in the country or territory outside New Zealand in which the non-resident visitor is resident or liable to tax on the basis of citizenship.
This section does not apply to the income of a public entertainer.

ird.aids.term.list: amount, exempt income, income, New Zealand, non-resident, non-resident visitor, public entertainer, resident in New Zealand, tax

prov.label: CW 22C
prov.heading: Amounts derived by non-residents from non-resident visitors in New Zealand

para.text: An amount of income derived by a non-resident person that has a source in New Zealand under section YD 4(2) or (3) (Classes of income treated as having New Zealand source) is exempt income if—
the income has a source in New Zealand only because a person who is a non-resident visitor is physically present in New Zealand; and
the amount derived is liable to tax in the country or territory outside New Zealand in which the non-resident person is resident or liable to tax on the basis of citizenship.
This section does not apply to the income of a public entertainer.

ird.aids.term.list: amount, exempt income, income, New Zealand, non-resident, non-resident visitor, public entertainer, tax

prov.label: CW 22D
prov.heading: Treatment of non-resident visitors for sections CW 22B and CW 22C

para.text: This section applies to an amount of income derived by a non-resident visitor from performing personal or professional services in New Zealand or an amount of income derived by a non-resident person that has a source in New Zealand under section YD 4(2) or (3) (Classes of income treated as having New Zealand source) if—

the amount is not liable to tax in a country or territory outside New Zealand in accordance with section CW 22B(1)﻿(b) or CW 22C(1)﻿(b), as applicable, because the non-resident visitor has ceased to be either resident in that country or territory or liable to tax on the basis of citizenship; and
section YD 1B(4) (Non-resident visitors) applies to the non-resident visitor; and

the laws of the country or territory outside New Zealand referred to in section YD 1B(4) treat the person’s cessation of residency or liability to tax as having occurred on an earlier date (the effective date) than the date the person stops being a non-resident visitor under that section (the cessation date); and
the amount is derived in the period starting on the effective date and ending on the cessation date.

prov.body.subprov.crosshead: Amount treated as liable to tax in foreign country or territory

para.text: For the purposes of section CW 22B(1)﻿(b) or CW 22C(1)﻿(b), as applicable, the amount derived is treated as being liable to tax in the country or territory outside New Zealand.

ird.aids.term.list: amount, income, New Zealand, non-resident, non-resident visitor, tax

prov.heading: Section CW 38 amended (Public authorities)

para.text: Repeal section CW 38(3).
After section CW 38(5)﻿(e), insert:
New Zealand Institute for Advanced Technology.

prov.heading: Section CW 38B amended (Public purpose Crown-controlled companies)

para.text: Repeal section CW 38B(3).

prov.heading: Section CW 39 amended (Local authorities)

para.text: Repeal section CW 39(3).

prov.heading: Section CW 55BC amended (Water organisations)

para.text: In section CW 55BC(2), replace In this section and section CW 39, water with Water.

prov.heading: New section CW 61B inserted (Income from supply of excess electricity from dwelling)

para.text: After section CW 61, insert:

prov.label: CW 61B
prov.heading: Income from supply of excess electricity from dwelling

para.text: An amount derived by a natural person from the supply of excess electricity generated at a dwelling occupied as a residence by the person is exempt income.
For the purposes of this section, excess electricity means electricity generated at a dwelling that is not consumed at the dwelling and is supplied to an electricity retailer.

ird.aids.term.list: amount, dwelling, excess electricity, exempt income, natural person

para.text: Subsection (1) applies for the 2026–27 and later income years.

prov.heading: Section CX 2 amended (Meaning of fringe benefit)

para.text: In section CX 2(1)﻿(b)﻿(i), replace CX 16 with CX 16B.

prov.heading: New section CX 16B inserted (Gift cards)

para.text: After section CX 16, insert:

prov.label: CX 16B
prov.heading: Gift cards

para.text: A fringe benefit arises when an employer provides a gift card to an employee, unless the employer chooses to treat it as an amount derived in connection with the employee’s employment under section CE 1 (Amounts derived in connection with employment).

prov.body.subprov.crosshead: When value of benefit is employment income

para.text: Despite subsection (1), if the provision of the card has a purpose or effect of defeating the application of the Child Support Act 1991, the value of the benefit is an amount derived in connection with the employee’s employment under section CE 1.

ird.aids.term.list: amount, employee, employer, employment, fringe benefit, gift card

prov.heading: Section CX 17 amended (Benefits provided to employees who are shareholders or investors)

para.text: Replace section CX 17(4)﻿(b) with:
in the absence of section CX 4, be, or be treated as, unclassified benefits under section RD 45 (Unclassified benefits and gift cards) if provided to a person in their capacity as an employee.

prov.heading: Section CX 24 amended (Benefits related to health or safety)

para.text: Replace section CX 24(c) with:
either—
would be excluded by section CX 23 from being a fringe benefit if provided on the employer’s premises; or
is personal protective equipment, including clothing, that is used or worn by a person to minimise risks to the person’s health and safety.
Subsection (1) applies for income years commencing on or after 1 April 2008.

prov.heading: Section CX 26 amended (Non-liable payments)

para.text: After section CX 26(2), insert:

amend.subprov.crosshead: Benefits provided to non-resident visitors

para.text: A benefit received by an employee who is a non-resident visitor is not a fringe benefit.
In section CX 26, list of defined terms, insert non-resident visitor and replace resident with resident in New Zealand.

prov.heading: Section CX 37 amended (Meaning of unclassified benefit)

para.text: In section CX 37(a), replace CX 16 with CX 16B.

prov.heading: Section CX 57B amended (Amounts derived during periods covered by calculation methods)

para.text: In section CX 57B(1), replace and cost method with cost method, and revenue account method.

prov.heading: New section CZ 42 inserted (Transitional tax relief: water organisations)

para.text: After section CZ 41, insert:

prov.label: CZ 42
prov.heading: Transitional tax relief: water organisations

para.text: This section applies to a water organisation that derives—
assessable income before the 2025–26 income year; and
exempt income under section CW 55BC (Water organisations) in the 2025–26 or a later income year.
No income tax liability arises under this Act for the water organisation solely because of the organisation’s income becoming exempt income under section CW 55BC.

ird.aids.term.list: assessable income, exempt income, income, income tax liability, income year, water organisation

para.text: Subsection (1) applies for the 2024–25 and 2025–26 income years.

prov.heading: Section DE 1 amended (What this subpart does)

para.text: In section DE 1(1), replace business purposes and partly for other purposes with business use and partly for other uses.
Subsection (1) applies for the 2008–09 and later income years.

prov.heading: Section DE 2B amended (Election to use kilometre rate method or costs method)

para.text: In section DE 2B(1)﻿(c), replace business purposes with business use.
Subsection (1) applies for the 2017–18 and later income years.

prov.heading: Section DE 5 amended (Actual records)

para.text: In section DE 5, replace a motor vehicle for business purposes with the motor vehicle for business use.
Subsection (1) applies for the 2008–09 and later income years.

prov.heading: Section DE 7 amended (Logbook requirements)

para.text: In section DE 7(1), replace business purposes with business use.
Subsection (1) applies for the 2008–09 and later income years.

prov.heading: Section DE 9 amended (Inadequate logbook)

para.text: In section DE 9(1), after proportion of business use, insert of a motor vehicle.
In section DE 9(1), replace use of a motor vehicle for business purposes with business use of the motor vehicle.
In section DE 9(2)﻿(a), replace use of the motor vehicle for business purposes with business use of the motor vehicle.
Subsections (1), (2), and (3) apply for the 2008–09 and later income years.

prov.heading: Section DE 10 amended (Variance during logbook term)

para.text: In section DE 10, replace average use of the motor vehicle for business purposes with average business use of the motor vehicle.
Subsection (1) applies for the 2008–09 and later income years.

prov.heading: Section DE 11 amended (Replacement vehicles)

para.text: In section DE 11(a), replace business purposes with business use.
Subsection (1) applies for the 2008–09 and later income years.

prov.heading: Section DE 12 amended (Kilometre rate method)

para.text: In section DE 12(2), replace business purposes with business use.
In section DE 12(3)﻿(b), replace business purposes and other purposes with business use and other uses.
Subsection (1) applies for the 2008–09 and later income years.
Subsection (2) applies for the 2017–18 and later income years.

prov.heading: Section DI 1 amended (New investment assets)

para.text: After section DI 1(2)﻿(c), insert:
section DI 4B provides for the treatment of certain items as assets:

prov.heading: Section DI 2 amended (When this subpart applies and does not apply)

para.text: Replace section DI 2(1), other than the heading, with:
This subpart applies for a person and a new investment asset acquired by them if, subject to subsection (1B), the following conditions are met:
if the asset is depreciable property, it first becomes available for use in New Zealand by the person on or after 22 May 2025; and
if the asset is not depreciable property, the person incurs expenditure in relation to it on or after 22 May 2025; and
the asset has never previously been used or available for use in New Zealand by any person for any purpose; and
the person has chosen to apply this subpart to the asset in a return of income for the income year.

amend.subprov.crosshead: Asset not used or available for use

para.text: For the purposes of subsection (1), an asset is not considered to have been used or available for use if the asset has been—
held only as trading stock:
used only in a manner necessary to prepare the asset for sale or exchange.
In section DI 2(2), replace an asset with a new investment asset.
In section DI 2, list of defined terms, insert new investment asset.

prov.heading: Section DI 3 replaced (Meaning of new asset transferee)

para.text: Replace section DI 3 with:

prov.label: DI 3
prov.heading: Meaning of new asset transferee

para.text: New asset transferee means a person who—
acquires an asset by way of transfer from another person (person A) who has previously chosen to apply this subpart and been allowed a deduction under section DI 5 for the asset; and
is treated as having been allowed the pre-transfer deductions that person A is allowed for amounts of depreciation loss under another provision of this Act, including—
section FB 21 (Depreciable property):
section FM 15(7) (Amortising property and revenue account property):
section FO 16(4) (Amortising property).

ird.aids.term.list: amount, deduction, depreciation loss

prov.heading: Section DI 4 amended (Meaning of new investment asset)

para.text: Replace section DI 4(a)﻿(viii) with:
an alteration, extension, or repair of an asset of the type in any of subparagraphs (i) to (vii) that increases its capital value:
In section DI 4, list of defined terms, insert acquire and delete improvement.

prov.heading: New section DI 4B inserted (Treatment of certain items as assets)

para.text: After section DI 4, insert:

prov.label: DI 4B
prov.heading: Treatment of certain items as assets

para.text: For the purposes of this subpart,—

an improvement for which a person is allowed a deduction under section DO 4, DO 12, or DP 3 (which relate to improvements to farm land, aquacultural business, or forestry land) is treated as an asset the person acquires and owns:
all petroleum development expenditure incurred by a person is treated as giving rise to an asset acquired and owned by the person:
all mining development expenditure incurred by a person is treated as giving rise to an asset acquired and owned by the person:

an alteration, extension, or repair of an asset of the type in any of section DI 4(a)﻿(i) to (vii) by a person is treated as an asset the person acquires and owns that is separate from the asset it alters, extends, or repairs.

ird.aids.term.list: deduction, mining development expenditure, petroleum development expenditure

prov.heading: Section DI 5 amended (New investment asset deduction)

para.text: In section DI 5(1), replace equal to the amount calculated by with for the amount calculated using.

prov.heading: Section DI 6 amended (Relationship to cost, calculations, etc, in other provisions)

para.text: Before section DI 6(1), insert the heading:

amend.subprov.crosshead: Other amounts reduced by amount of section DI 5 deduction

para.text: In section DI 6(1), after section DI 5 for, insert expenditure incurred in acquiring.
In section DI 6, insert as subsection (2):

amend.subprov.crosshead: Available amount of section DI 5 deduction

para.text: For the purposes of subsection (1), if the amount of person A’s deduction is less than the amount (the available deduction amount) they would have been allowed if they had used the asset wholly in deriving assessable income or carrying on a business for the purpose of deriving assessable income, the applicable amount referred to in subsection (1) is reduced by the available deduction amount for the purposes of quantifying the amount of depreciation loss under subpart EE or the amount of a deduction under subpart DO, DP, DT, or DU, as applicable.
In section DI 6, replace the Examples with:

ABC company purchases a new investment asset (Asset A) for $10,000 on 1 October 2025. Asset A is depreciable property and has a DV rate of 10%. Asset A is used for 6 months in the income year ending 31 March 2026. In the 2025–26 income year, ABC company is allowed the following deduction for the new investment asset under section DI 5:

Section DI 6(1)﻿(a) ensures that the cost base for the asset for depreciation purposes is reduced by the amount of the deduction under section DI 5, that is, $10,000 − $2,000 = $8,000. Depreciation for the asset is then calculated for the 2025–26 income year as follows:
The total deduction for Asset A in the 2025–26 income year is $2,400. The adjusted tax value of the asset for the 2026–27 income year will be $7,600 ($8,000 − $400).

ABC Company is in a consolidated group with DEF Company. At the end of the 2025–26 income year, ABC Company transfers Asset A to DEF Company. Section FM 15(7) applies to treat DEF Company as if it has claimed the $400 depreciation loss already claimed by ABC Company. Section DI 6 operates to transfer the $2,000 new investment asset deduction claimed by ABC Company to DEF Company for the purposes of the item new investment asset amount in section EE 48(1B) and (1C) and also operates to ensure the opening value of Asset A for DEF Company for depreciation purposes is $7,600. This means that if DEF Company were to dispose of Asset A at a later date for more than this amount, DEF Company would have depreciation recovery income.
In section DI 6, list of defined terms, insert assessable income, consolidated group, depreciation recovery income, dispose, and income year.

amend.subprov.crosshead: Ring-fencing rule for loss calculated under revenue account method

para.text: The deduction for a FIF loss calculated under the revenue account method is subject to the ring-fencing rule in section DN 8B.

prov.heading: New section DN 8B inserted (Ring-fencing cap on deduction: revenue account method)

para.text: After section DN 8, insert:

prov.label: DN 8B
prov.heading: Ring-fencing cap on deduction: revenue account method

para.text: This section applies when a person has an amount of FIF loss under section EX 56B(3)﻿(b) (Revenue account method) for an income year.
The amount of deduction the person is allowed in the income year for the FIF loss is limited to the amount of FIF income of the person under section EX 56B in the income year.
Any excess FIF loss not able to be deducted because of subsection (2) is a RAM net loss able to be carried forward and used under section EX 59B (Ring-fencing RAM net losses).

prov.heading: Section DV 27 amended (Employee share schemes)

para.text: In section DV 27(6), after or loss, insert , arising on the share scheme taxing date,.
In section DV 27, list of defined terms, insert share scheme taxing date.

Subsections (1) and (2) apply to an employee share scheme to the extent the share scheme taxing date under section CE 7B(1) of the Income Tax Act 2007 arises on or after 1 April 2026. Subsections (1) and (2) do not apply to a person in respect of an employee share scheme that is part of a merger or acquisition transaction for which the person entered into a binding contract before the date on which the Taxation (Annual Rates for 2025–26, Compliance Simplification, and Remedial Measures) Act 2026 receives the Royal assent.

prov.heading: New section EA 4B inserted (Deferred tax for unlisted employee share schemes)

para.text: After section EA 4, insert:

prov.label: EA 4B
prov.heading: Deferred tax for unlisted employee share schemes

para.text: This section applies to shares issued or transferred under an employee share scheme by a company that is not—
a listed company:
a member of a group of companies where at least 1 member is a listed company.

The employer may, at the time the shares are issued or transferred, designate the shares issued or transferred to an employee share scheme beneficiary as employee deferred shares. If the employer makes that designation, the employer must notify both the Commissioner and the employee share scheme beneficiary of that designation within 20 days after the date of issue or transfer, or by a later date allowed by the Commissioner.

If the employer chooses to designate shares as employee deferred shares under subsection (2), the share scheme taxing date for those shares is deferred to the liquidity event date in accordance with section CE 7B(3) (Meaning of share scheme taxing date).
Liquidity event date,—
subject to paragraph (b), means the earliest of the following dates:
the date on which the company that issued or transferred the shares under the employee share scheme referred to in subsection (1) becomes a listed company; and

the date the employee share scheme beneficiary sells or transfers the shares to a person who is not associated with a beneficiary described in section CE 7(a)﻿(i) or (ii) (Meaning of employee share scheme); and
the date the shares are cancelled, including by the company ceasing to exist:
if the date determined under paragraph (a) occurs while the shares are subject to a restriction that prevents their disposal, means the date on which the restriction ends:

does not include a date referred to in paragraph (a) or (b) if the shares are sold or otherwise exchanged only for shares in a company that would satisfy subsection (1) if it had issued the shares under an employee share scheme.

para.text: Subsection (1) applies to shares issued or transferred by a company under an employee share scheme on or after 1 April 2026.

A variable principal debt instrument is an excepted financial arrangement if the total value on every day in an income year for all variable principal debt instruments to which a person is a party is $100,000 or less, except when the person who is a party to the financial arrangement—
makes an election under section EW 8; or
is a company.

A person is a cash basis person for an income year if 1 or both of the following applies in the person’s case for the income year:
section EW 57(1):
section EW 57(2).

prov.heading: Section EW 57 amended (Thresholds)

para.text: In section EW 57(1), replace EW 54(1)﻿(a)﻿(i) with EW 54(1)﻿(a).
In section EW 57(1), replace 100,000 with 200,000.
In section EW 57(2), replace EW 54(1)﻿(a)﻿(ii) with EW 54(1)﻿(b).
In section EW 57(2), replace 1,000,000 with 2,000,000.
After section EW 57(2)﻿(c), insert:

for a financial arrangement denominated in a foreign currency, the value calculated by converting the value of the arrangement into New Zealand dollars at the exchange rate applying on the date the person first became a party to the arrangement and,—

if the principal amount of the arrangement increases, the value of the arrangement is calculated by converting the amount of the increase into New Zealand dollars at the exchange rate applying on the date of the change and adding it to the original New Zealand dollar value of the arrangement:

if the principal amount of the arrangement decreases, the value of the arrangement is calculated by converting the amount of the reduction into New Zealand dollars at the exchange rate applying on the date the person first became a party to the arrangement and subtracting it from the original New Zealand dollar value.

para.text: In section EW 58(1), replace EW 57(1) to (3) with EW 57(1) and (2).
In section EW 58(4), replace EW 57(1) to (3) with EW 57(1) and (2).

In section EW 60(3), replace EW 54(1)﻿(a) and (b) with EW 54(1).

prov.heading: Section EX 29 amended (Attributing interests in FIFs)

para.text: In section EX 29(1)﻿(b), replace EX 43 with EX 43B.

prov.heading: New section EX 43B inserted (Exemption for share users in returning share transfers)

para.text: After section EX 43, insert:

prov.label: EX 43B
prov.heading: Exemption for share users in returning share transfers

para.text: This section applies when a share user—
acquires an original share under a returning share transfer; and
disposes of the original share to a person other than the share supplier or a person associated with the share supplier; and
acquires an identical share to return to the share supplier under the returning share transfer.
The share user’s rights in the original share and the identical share are not attributing interests in a FIF in an income year.
Subsection (2) does not apply if—
the share user is related to the share supplier:
the returning share transfer is, or is part of, a structured arrangement.

para.text: Subsection (1) applies to a returning share transfer entered into on or after 1 April 2026.

prov.heading: Section EX 44 amended (Five calculation methods)

para.text: In the heading to section EX 44, replace Five with Six.
After section EX 44(1)﻿(f), insert:
the revenue account method.
In section EX 44(2), after EX 46,, insert EX 46B,.
In section EX 44, list of defined terms, insert revenue account method.

prov.heading: Section EX 46 amended (Limits on choice of calculation methods)

para.text: In section EX 46(1)﻿(b),—
after EX 47,, insert EX 47B,; and
after or section, insert EX 46B,.
After section EX 46(9), insert:

amend.subprov.crosshead: Revenue account method

para.text: A person may use the revenue account method to calculate FIF income or loss from an attributing interest in a FIF only if section EX 46B provides that the person may use the revenue account method.
In section EX 46, list of defined terms,—
insert revenue account method; and
delete accounting profits method, generally accepted accounting practice, grey list company, IFRS 9, PIE, share supplier, and shareholder.

prov.heading: New section EX 46B inserted (Limits on choice of revenue account method)

para.text: After section EX 46, insert:

prov.label: EX 46B
prov.heading: Limits on choice of revenue account method

para.text: This section sets out when a person may choose to use the revenue account method to calculate FIF income or loss from an attributing interest in a FIF for an income year.
A person may use the revenue account method to calculate FIF income or loss from an attributing interest in a FIF only if—
either—
the person is a RAM taxpayer and the interest is a RAM interest; or
the person is an extended RAM taxpayer and the interest is an extended RAM interest; and

the person chooses to use the revenue account method for the income year in which the person first meets the tests in section CQ 5 (When FIF income arises) or DN 6 (When FIF loss arises) for a RAM interest or extended RAM interest, as applicable, ignoring all excluded RAM interests, and for each later income year; and
the person uses the revenue account method to calculate FIF income or loss for all their RAM interests or extended RAM interests, as applicable, other than their excluded RAM interests.

prov.body.subprov.crosshead: Revenue account method for previously held interests

para.text: Despite subsection (2), a person may also use the revenue account method to calculate FIF income or loss from an attributing interest in a FIF if—
the person is a New Zealand resident who is not treated as non-resident under a double tax agreement and is not a transitional resident (a returning resident); and
the person was non-resident, or treated as non-resident under a double tax agreement, before becoming a returning resident; and
immediately before the person became non-resident, or treated as non-resident under a double tax agreement, the person was a RAM taxpayer; and
when the person was a RAM taxpayer, the interest was a RAM interest for which the person chose to use the revenue account method to calculate FIF income or loss; and
the interest is a RAM interest; and

the person chooses to use the revenue account method for the income year in which the person first meets the tests in section CQ 5 or DN 6 after the person becomes a returning resident and for each later income year; and
the person uses the revenue account method to calculate FIF income or loss for—
all interests that satisfy this subsection; and
all other interests for which the person may choose to use the revenue account method, if any.

prov.body.subprov.crosshead: Revenue account method for certain transferred interests

para.text: Despite subsection (2), a person may also use the revenue account method to calculate FIF income or loss from an attributing interest in a FIF if—
the person is a natural person; and

the person acquires the interest from a RAM taxpayer or an extended RAM taxpayer in circumstances to which subpart FB (Transfers of relationship property) or section FC 3 (Property transferred to spouse, civil union partner, or de facto partner) apply; and
the interest was a RAM interest for the RAM taxpayer or an extended RAM interest for the extended RAM taxpayer, as applicable; and
the RAM taxpayer or extended RAM taxpayer, as applicable, chose to use the revenue account method to calculate FIF income or loss for the interest; and
if the interest was a RAM interest for a RAM taxpayer, the interest satisfies the criteria set out in paragraph (a) of the definition of RAM interest in subsection (6), ignoring subparagraph (ii).

prov.body.subprov.crosshead: Revenue account method for previously eligible RAM interests

para.text: Despite subsection (2), if a person holds an attributing interest in a FIF that is a RAM interest for which they use the revenue account method to calculate the FIF income or loss of the interest and that interest ceases to be a RAM interest, the person may continue to use the revenue account method to calculate the FIF income or loss of that interest.
RAM interest, for a person,—
means an attributing interest in a FIF if—
the interest is a share in a foreign company; and

the share was acquired by the person before, or as the result of a contract entered into before, they became a New Zealand resident, including a transitional resident, who is not treated as non-resident under a double tax agreement; and
the share is not listed on a recognised exchange; and
no effective redemption facility for market value is available to the person for the share; and
less than 80% of the foreign company’s value is from shares that would not satisfy subparagraph (iii) or (iv); and
includes an attributing interest in a FIF that satisfies the criteria set out in paragraph (a), ignoring subparagraph (ii), if—
the person was an extended RAM taxpayer; and
the interest was an extended RAM interest of the person; and
the person ceased to be an extended RAM taxpayer; and
the person continues to hold the interest.
Extended RAM interest, for a person who is an extended RAM taxpayer, means an attributing interest in a FIF—
that is a share in a foreign company; and

any disposal of which is liable to tax under the laws of the country or territory outside New Zealand in which the person is liable to tax on the basis of citizenship or a right to work or live in that country or territory, being a country or territory with which New Zealand has a double tax agreement.
Excluded RAM interest, for a person, means a RAM interest or extended RAM interest for which the person—
chooses to use the attributable FIF income method under section EX 46(3); or
is required to use the comparative value method or deemed rate of return method, as applicable, under section EX 47 or EX 47B.
RAM taxpayer means—
a natural person who—
was non-resident or treated as non-resident under a double tax agreement for a continuous period of at least 5 years immediately before becoming New Zealand resident; and
became New Zealand resident and not treated as non-resident under a double tax agreement on or after 1 April 2024; and
is New Zealand resident and not treated as non-resident under a double tax agreement and is not a transitional resident; or
the trustee of a trust that—
has no gifting settlor who is not a natural person or deceased person; and
at all times in the income year, is a complying trust for a distribution made at the time; and

is mainly for the benefit of 1 or more natural persons for whom the gifting settlors of the trust have natural love and affection (or had natural love and affection when alive) or is mainly for the benefit of an organisation or trust with income that is exempt income under section CW 41 or CW 42 (which relate to the income of charities); and
is not a superannuation scheme; and
has a principal settlor that satisfies the criteria set out in paragraph (a) at the time the trust chooses to use the revenue account method in accordance with subsection (2)﻿(b).
Extended RAM taxpayer means—
a natural person who—
was non-resident or treated as non-resident under a double tax agreement for a continuous period of at least 5 years immediately before becoming New Zealand resident; and
became New Zealand resident and not treated as non-resident under a double tax agreement on or after 1 April 2024; and

is liable to tax in a country or territory outside New Zealand on the basis of citizenship or a right to work or live in that country or territory, being a country or territory with which New Zealand has a double tax agreement; or
a natural person who—
was previously an extended RAM taxpayer; and

is liable to tax in a country or territory outside New Zealand on the basis of citizenship or a right to work or live in that country or territory, being a country or territory with which New Zealand has a double tax agreement; or
the trustee of a trust that—
satisfies the criteria set out in paragraph (b)﻿(i) to (iv) of the definition of RAM taxpayer in subsection (9); and
has a principal settlor that satisfies the criteria set out in paragraph (a) or (b) of this definition.

prov.body.subprov.crosshead: Transitional provision for transitional residents before 1 April 2024

para.text: For the purposes of subsections (9)﻿(a)﻿(ii) and (10)﻿(a)﻿(ii), a person is treated as becoming New Zealand resident on or after 1 April 2024 if the person became New Zealand resident before that date but was a transitional resident and only stopped being a transitional resident on or after 1 April 2024.

ird.aids.term.list: amount, attributing interest, comparative value method, complying trust, deemed rate of return method, double tax agreement, excluded RAM interest, exempt income, extended RAM interest, extended RAM taxpayer, FIF, FIF income, FIF loss, foreign company, gifting settlor, income, income year, loss, natural person, New Zealand, New Zealand resident, non-resident, principal settlor, RAM interest, RAM taxpayer, recognised exchange, revenue account method, share, superannuation scheme, tax, transitional resident, trustee

prov.heading: Section EX 51 amended (Comparative value method)

para.text: After section EX 51(8), insert:

amend.subprov.crosshead: Treatment of attributing interests subject to returning share transfer

para.text: For a person using the comparative value method to calculate FIF income for an attributing interest in a FIF that is an original share subject to a returning share transfer, the attributing interest is treated as being held by the share supplier, except if—
the share user is related to the share supplier:
the returning share transfer is, or is part of, a structured arrangement.
In section EX 51, list of defined terms, insert original share, related, returning share transfer, share supplier, share user, and structured arrangement.
Subsections (1) and (2) apply to a returning share transfer entered into on or after 1 April 2026.

prov.heading: Section EX 53 amended (Fair dividend rate periodic method)

para.text: In section EX 53(16C), replace fair dividend rate annual method with fair dividend rate periodic method.
In section EX 53, list of defined terms, delete fair dividend rate annual method.
Subsections (1) and (2) apply for income years beginning on or after 1 July 2018.

prov.heading: Section EX 55 amended (Deemed rate of return method)

para.text: Replace section EX 55(4)﻿(b) with:
deemed rate is the rate set by a determination made under section 91AAP of the Tax Administration Act 1994 for this section for the relevant income year.
Replace section EX 55(6)﻿(c) with:
deemed rate is the rate set by a determination made under section 91AAP of the Tax Administration Act 1994 for this section for the relevant income year:
Repeal section EX 55(15).

prov.heading: New section EX 56B inserted (Revenue account method)

para.text: After section EX 56, insert:

prov.label: EX 56B
prov.heading: Revenue account method

para.text: This section applies if a person is using the revenue account method to calculate FIF income or loss from an attributing interest in a FIF for an income year.

The amount of any dividends derived by the person from the interest in the income year, including any foreign withholding tax or other amount that the person is allowed as a credit under section LE 1 (Tax credits for imputation credits) or LJ 2 (Tax credits for foreign income tax) in relation to those dividends, is FIF income of the person for the income year.

prov.body.subprov.crosshead: FIF income or loss on disposal

para.text: If the person disposes of the interest in the income year, the amount (the net disposal amount) calculated under subsection (4) or (10) is—
FIF income of the person if the amount is a positive number:
FIF loss of the person if the amount is a negative number.
The net disposal amount is the amount calculated using the formula—
In the formula in subsection (4),—

disposal proceeds is the amount received by the person from disposing, or being treated as disposing, of the interest, including any foreign withholding tax or other amount that the person is allowed as a credit under section LJ 2 in relation to that amount received, determined under subsection (6):
cost is the amount of the expenditure the person incurs in acquiring the interest for the purpose of applying the revenue account method under this section:

foreign accruals is the total amount of any gains or losses from the interest determined under subsections (7) and (8) for periods when the person held the interest as a non-resident or a New Zealand resident who is treated as a non-resident under a double tax agreement or is a transitional resident starting on or after the date the person acquires the interest for the purpose of applying the revenue account method under this section.
For the purposes of subsections (5)﻿(a) and (11)﻿(a), the disposal proceeds is,—

if the person has disposed of the interest during the income year, the amount received by the person from disposing of the interest, unless section GC 4 (Disposals and acquisitions of FIF attributing interests) applies; or

if the person has not disposed of the interest but is treated as having disposed of the interest during the income year, including under any of subsection (12) or section EX 63(2), (6), or (8), EX 65(6), EX 66B(2), or GC 4, the amount the person is treated as having received for the disposal under the relevant section.

For the purpose of subsection (5)﻿(c), the foreign accruals for an interest for a period the person held the interest as a non-resident or a New Zealand resident who is treated as a non-resident under a double tax agreement or is a transitional resident is,—

if the period is the period in which the person acquired the interest before first becoming a New Zealand resident who is not treated as a non-resident under a double tax agreement and is not a transitional resident (a RAM resident), the market value of the interest on the date (the first residence date) the person first becomes a RAM resident less the cost of the interest; or

if the period is any other period in which the person is a non-resident between periods of being a RAM resident, the market value of the interest on the date (the residence date) the person becomes a RAM resident at the end of that period of non-residence less the market value of the interest on the date (the non-residence date) the person became a non-resident at the beginning of that period.

prov.body.subprov.crosshead: When market value must be obtained

para.text: For the purpose of subsection (7), the market value of the interest must be obtained before the later of—
the due date of the return for the income year in which the first residence date, the residence date, or the non-residence date, as applicable, falls; and
12 months from the first residence date, the residence date, or the non-residence date, as applicable.
Despite subsection (4), the net disposal amount is the amount calculated using the formula in subsection (10) if the interest is not a share that is listed on a recognised exchange and—
the person is unable to obtain a market value of the interest before the relevant date in subsection (8); or
the market value of the interest cannot be determined except by independent valuation and the person chooses not to obtain an independent valuation.
The net disposal amount is the amount calculated using the formula—

eqn.eqn-line: ((disposal proceeds − cost) ÷ ownership period × FIF period) × 0.7.

para.text: In the formula in subsection (10),—

disposal proceeds is the amount received by the person from disposing, or being treated as disposing, of the interest, including any foreign withholding tax or other amount that the person is allowed as a credit under section LJ 2 in relation to that amount received, determined under subsection (6):
cost is the amount of the expenditure the person incurs in acquiring the interest:
ownership period is the number of days in the period that starts on the day the person acquires the interest and ends on the day the person disposes of the interest:

FIF period is the number of days in the period that starts on the day the person first uses the revenue account method to calculate FIF income or loss for the interest and ends on the day the person disposes of the interest, excluding the number of days in that period on which the person is a non-resident.

prov.body.subprov.crosshead: Treatment as disposal on leaving New Zealand

para.text: Subject to subsection (16), if the person ceases to be New Zealand resident, the person is treated as having disposed of the interest immediately before the change in residence for an amount equal to its market value at that time.

Subsection (14) applies if the person has an amount of FIF income or loss under subsection (3) for an income year (the departure year) that arises because the person is treated as having disposed of the interest under subsection (12).

prov.body.subprov.crosshead: Suspended recognition of FIF income or loss

para.text: The amount of FIF income or loss is an amount of income or loss under section CQ 5 (When FIF income arises) or DN 6 (When FIF loss arises) for the person for the departure year only if the person actually disposes of the interest before the earlier of—
the date the person becomes New Zealand resident again; and
the date that is 3 years from the date the person ceased to be New Zealand resident.

If subsection (14) applies and the disposal referred to in that subsection takes place in an income year (the disposal year) that is later than the departure year, despite the time bar, the Commissioner may amend the person’s assessment for the departure year to the extent to which it relates to the person’s liability for tax on the FIF income or loss arising under subsection (3) at any time in the period that ends 4 years after the end of the disposal year.

prov.body.subprov.crosshead: No treatment as disposal if no actual disposal

para.text: The person is not treated as having disposed of the interest under subsection (12) if the person does not actually dispose of the interest before the earlier of—
the date the person becomes New Zealand resident again; and
the date that is 3 years from the date the person ceased to be New Zealand resident.

prov.body.subprov.crosshead: Treatment of attributing interests subject to returning share transfer

para.text: For a person using the revenue account method to calculate FIF income or loss for an attributing interest in a FIF that is an original share subject to a returning share transfer, the attributing interest is treated as being held by the share supplier, except if—
the share user is related to the share supplier:
the returning share transfer is, or is part of, a structured arrangement.

prov.body.subprov.crosshead: Dividends and disposals for extended RAM taxpayers

para.text: For the purposes of subsections (2) and (3) and a person who is an extended RAM taxpayer, if, in the absence of this subsection, the person would have an amount of FIF income or loss under subsection (2) or (3) from deriving a dividend from, or disposing of, the interest,—

the person is only treated as having derived a dividend from, or disposed of, the interest if the laws of the country or territory outside New Zealand in which the person is liable to tax in accordance with section EX 46B(10)﻿(a)﻿(iii) treats the person as having derived a dividend from, or disposed of, the interest; and
the laws of that other country or territory are applied to determine whether the person has derived a dividend from, or disposed of, the interest.
